rule.desc: Define and honor nullness contracts for public APIs.
Use `Optional<T>` for return values that may be absent.
Prefer primitives over boxed types unless nullability or collections require boxing.
Override `equals` and `hashCode` together.
Ensure `compareTo` is consistent with `equals`.
Use records for simple, immutable data carriers (Java 16+).
Organize packages by feature, not by technical layer.
Define interfaces at system boundaries; keep implementations package-private.
Use the smallest visibility that works; avoid public fields.
Use builders when a constructor has more than 4 parameters.
Prefer named static factories over overloaded constructors.
Utility classes must be `final` with a private constructor.
Use unchecked exceptions for programmer errors; use checked exceptions only when callers can realistically recover.
Use try-with-resources for `AutoCloseable` types.
Wrap exceptions with a meaningful message and original cause.
Catch the most specific exception type possible.
Log errors at boundaries (API, job runner) and avoid re-logging the same exception.
Validate external inputs at the boundary and fail fast.
Prefer immutable objects and defensive copies across threads.
Use `java.util.concurrent` utilities instead of manual synchronization.
Keep synchronized sections minimal and lock on private objects.
Use `Atomic*` for simple counters; use `synchronized` or locks for compound invariants.
Document whether a class is thread-safe or not.
Pick collections based on access patterns and size.
Use `StringBuilder` for repeated concatenation in loops.
Use streams for clear, side-effect-free pipelines. Prefer loops in hot paths.
Use primitive types and primitive streams in tight loops.
Profile before making performance changes.
Prefer `@BeforeEach` for fixtures; avoid mutable shared state.
Tests must not depend on execution order or shared state.
Mock external systems (DB/HTTP/clock). Do not mock value objects or simple DTOs.
Avoid wall-clock time, random, and sleep in tests.
Separate unit and integration tests with tags or naming.
Use JUnit 5 (`org.junit.jupiter`) as the default test runtime.
Use descriptive test names that state behavior and context.
Structure tests with Arrange/Act/Assert or Given/When/Then.
Use `@ParameterizedTest` for behavior with multiple inputs.
Prefer fluent assertions (AssertJ). If not available, use JUnit assertions consistently.
Use `assertThrows` and assert on message or fields.
Prefer Robolectric for JVM tests; use instrumented tests only when device APIs are required.
Use AndroidX Test Runner for instrumented tests.
Use Espresso for UI behavior, but keep UI tests minimal.
Use Idling Resources for async synchronization.

tens.desc: Spring test slices and context usage.
tens.label: Spring Testing

rule.desc: Prefer test slices (`@WebMvcTest`, `@DataJpaTest`) over full context tests.
Use `@SpringBootTest` only for true integration tests.
Use `@MockBean` for external collaborators, not for simple value objects.
Use `@TestConfiguration` or `@Import` to provide test-only beans.
Avoid dynamic properties that bust Spring context caching.
Returning `null` for missing values.
Using `Optional` as a method parameter or field.
Returning `null` instead of empty collections.
Using boxed `Boolean` where `boolean` suffices.
Calling `Optional.get()` without checking presence.
Catching `Exception` or `Throwable` broadly.
Empty catch blocks or ignoring errors.
Throwing a new exception without the original cause.
Using exceptions for normal control flow.
Sharing mutable state across threads without coordination.
Synchronizing on `this`, strings, or other public objects.
Double-checked locking without `volatile`.
Creating threads directly and forgetting to shut them down.
Optimizing without evidence of a bottleneck.
Using streams with side effects or shared mutable state.
Using `List.contains` in hot paths with large lists.
Compiling regex patterns repeatedly in loops.
Tests rely on execution order or shared mutable state.
Mocking internals, value objects, or too many collaborators.
Multiple unrelated assertions without clear intent.
Catching exceptions in tests without failing.
Tests depend on real timezones or `System.currentTimeMillis()`.
Using `Thread.sleep` for synchronization.
Non-seeded randomness or data from external services.
